bundle_name: M4 closure -- broker-payload fixture-replay tests for IBKR + Tastytrade
theme: Closes M4 from the v0.1.64 Red Team review of v0.1.63 R1. The IBKR + Tastytrade get_net_liquidation parsers had no recorded fixtures so a broker-side schema change (renamed key, dropped wrapper, type flip from string to float) would silently break parsing -- the drift detector would receive None on every poll and classify every tick as no_broker_data, effectively disabling drift detection without an obvious failure mode. v0.1.68 adds canonical recorded payload fixtures + replay tests with positive (parse to expected net-liq) and negative (missing key, dropped wrapper, non-numeric value, malformed HTTP response) coverage. The 'MUST NOT raise' guarantee of the BrokerEquityAdapter Protocol is also pinned: every malformed-input path returns None gracefully.
modules_edited: tests/fixtures/broker_payloads/ibkr_account_summary.json: NEW canonical IBKR Client Portal /portfolio/{accountId}/summary response. Pins the {amount: float, currency: str} drill-down schema for netliquidation / equitywithloanvalue / totalcashvalue / availablefunds. Includes _provenance metadata block with endpoint URL, IBKR docs link, expected net-liq.

tests/fixtures/broker_payloads/tastytrade_balance.json: NEW canonical Tastytrade /accounts/{id}/balances response. Pins the {data: {...}, context: str} wrapper layer + hyphenated keys + string-typed decimals (cash-balance, equity-buying-power, net-liquidating-value).

tests/test_broker_payload_fixtures.py: NEW 19 tests across TestIbkrFixtureReplay (8), TestTastyFixtureReplay (9), TestParsersStillSatisfyProtocol (2). Replays canonical fixtures for happy-path parse + drives negative cases (missing key, type flip, dropped wrapper, malformed HTTP, no creds) to assert the parser returns None instead of raising or producing garbage.
deferred_followups: B3 BLOCKER -- tier-A aggregate equity invariant (needs design call on per-account vs per-fleet aggregation)
H4 -- server-side broker payload timestamps (parse server-reported ts to age TTL on broker-clock not poll-cycle)
M1 -- per-bot drift detection (multi-account scope expansion)
M2 -- KillVerdict synthesis on sustained drift (gated on H1 calibrator empirics)
L1 -- adapter name uniqueness (accepted as not-a-bug under single-account today)
milestone_title: M4 closure -- canonical IBKR + Tastytrade payload fixtures + 19 replay tests pin the get_net_liquidation parser contract. Broker-side schema drift now surfaces as a CI failure rather than a silent live-runtime regression.
